stories.title: US Open Final Round — Clark Holds 6-Shot Lead, Final Group Tees Off at 2:30 PM ET
stories.angle: Final round of the 2026 US Open at Shinnecock Hills, Southampton, NY. Wyndham Clark enters the final round with a commanding six-shot lead — his third-round 70 (even par) extending his advantage to what is historically an insurmountable margin. Clark and Scheffler are in the final pairing, teeing off at 2:30 PM ET. Rory McIlroy fell back sharply on Saturday (four bogeys in six holes on the back nine), and now trails Clark by 8 shots. Clark, who won the 2023 US Open, is bidding for his second major. Only Greg Norman at the 1996 Masters has blown a 6-shot lead entering a major's final round.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: US Open Final Round — TODAY. Wyndham Clark leads by SIX shots. Final group: Clark + Scheffler, 2:30 PM ET at Shinnecock Hills. 21 players have led by 6+ entering a major final round. Only Greg Norman (1996 Masters) lost. #USOpen #Golf

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Clark won the 2023 US Open. He leads by six today. Only Shinnecock Hills can stop him now — the most demanding course in American golf. Tee time 2:30 PM ET. Don't miss this. #USOpen #WyndhamClark

article.headline: 2026 US Open Final Round: Clark's Six-Shot Lead and the History That Backs Him Up

stories.title: Scottie Scheffler's 30th Birthday Grand Slam Bid — One Round, All the Stakes
stories.angle: Sunday June 21 is Scottie Scheffler's 30th birthday — and Father's Day. He's already won the Masters, the PGA Championship, and The Open Championship. The US Open is the only major he's missing for a career Grand Slam. He's 6 shots behind Clark — a deficit that has been overcome before (though rarely). Three consecutive birdies on Nos. 14-16 on Saturday showed his game is sharp. The storyline: can the world's No. 1 player erase history, close 6 shots, and complete the Grand Slam on his birthday/Father's Day? ESPN's headline: "If anyone can catch Clark, it's Scheffler."

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Today: Scottie Scheffler's 30th birthday. Father's Day. 6 shots back at Shinnecock. Career Grand Slam on the line. He's already won The Masters, PGA Championship and The Open. The US Open is all that's left. This is the stuff of legend. #USOpen #Scheffler

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Scheffler made 3 consecutive birdies on Nos. 14-16 at Shinnecock in R3. His game is sharp. 6 shots back. His birthday. Father's Day. ESPN: "If anyone can catch Clark, it's Scheffler." Can he? #USOpen #GrandSlam

article.headline: Scottie Scheffler's 30th Birthday: Grand Slam, Father's Day, and Six Shots to Erase at Shinnecock

stories.title: Meijer LPGA Classic Final Round — Jing Yan Leads by 1 Chasing First Tour Win
stories.angle: Final round at Blythefield Country Club in Belmont, Michigan. Jing Yan leads at -14 (202) by 1 shot over England's Lottie Woad, with Cassie Porter 2 shots back. Yan is chasing her first LPGA Tour title. Woad is playing the last event before the KPMG Women's PGA Championship (June 25-28). Wind conditions made Saturday's round challenging (R3 described as "wind-swept"). Final round drama: unknown quantity Yan vs. experienced contenders Woad and Porter.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Meijer LPGA Classic final round: Jing Yan leads Lottie Woad by 1 shot (-14 vs. -13). Yan seeks her first LPGA Tour title. Woad uses it as KPMG prep. Both tee off on a Sunday loaded with drama. #LPGA #MeijerLPGA

article.headline: Meijer LPGA Classic Final Round: Jing Yan Leads by One, Woad Right Behind With Major Momentum on the Line

stories.title: KPMG Women's PGA Preview — Korda Eyes Three-Peat at Hazeltine Starting June 25
stories.angle: The KPMG Women's PGA Championship begins June 25 at Hazeltine National Golf Club in Chaska, Minnesota (purse: $12 million). Nelly Korda, the defending champion (two-time winner), enters as the marquee name. The field includes 11 past champions. The Meijer LPGA Classic winner earns the final field spot — giving this week's LPGA final round additional context.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: KPMG Women's PGA Championship preview: June 25-28 at Hazeltine National. $12M purse. Nelly Korda eyes a three-peat. 11 past champions in the field. The Meijer LPGA Classic winner earns the final spot. Big week ahead. #KPMG #LPGA

stories.title: US Open Fade — McIlroy Falls Back, Grillo's Low Round, Sunday's Other Storylines
stories.angle: Despite a promising start to Saturday's round (2-under through the turn), Rory McIlroy made four bogeys in six holes on the back nine at Shinnecock to fall 8 shots back of Clark. The 2011 US Open champion's hopes of a second Shinnecock major faded quickly. Emiliano Grillo shot the low round of Saturday at 67 (-3) to move up the leaderboard. Matt Fitzpatrick and Xander Schauffele are also in the mix. Clark still needs to execute — Shinnecock Hills is a notoriously unforgiving course in final-round pressure situations.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Rory McIlroy was 2-under through 9 holes on Saturday at Shinnecock. Then: 4 bogeys in 6 back nine holes. The 2011 US Open champion now trails Clark by 8. Shinnecock showed its teeth. #USOpen #McIlroy

article.headline: US Open Moving Day: McIlroy's Back-Nine Collapse, Grillo's 67, and Shinnecock's Cruelty
